title: Uttar Pradesh government scraps smart prepaid meter system; shifts all consumers to postpaid mode
content: Consumers will receive electricity bills after consumption, with bills for electricity used in May 2026 to be issued in June under the postpaid system

title: West Bengal government formation LIVE: Suvendu Adhikari elected new CM of Bengal, to take oath on May 9
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah, who presided over BJP legislature party meeting said that all the proposals were received in favour of Suvendu Adhikari.

title: From political defection to murder: Jayakanthan’s story on shifting loyalties
content: Jayakanthan writes that the poor, who become victims of politicians’ machinations, live with hungry stomachs, stoves that rarely burn and huts without roofs

title: Telangana Govt issues detailed guidelines for Musi rejuvenation Phase-I; December 2027 deadline for project completion
content: Telangana Cabinet sub-committee on May 8, 2026, directs officials to start construction work of the initial phase spanning five km within 21 km stretch from Himayatsagar to Bapughat and Osmansagar to Bapughat with minimal impact

title: Tamil Nadu government formation LIVE: CPI, CPI(M) extend unconditional support to Vijay's TVK
content: Viduthalai Chiruthaigal Katchi that has won two seats is also likely to join the alliance with Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam, sources told The Hindu.

title: Final decision on Kerala CM turns into political cliff-hanger as ball now in Congress High Command’s court
content: The question of who among the three probables – V.D. Satheesan, K.C. Venugopal and Ramesh Chennithala – will make the cut appears set to go down to the wire, with High Command reportedly scheduled to make pivotal announcement on Sunday

title: CBI files chargesheet against Shubhkamna Buildtech and others
content: The CBI is currently investigating 50 cases registered pursuant to the directions of the Supreme Court against various builder companies and financial institutions across the country in matters concerning cheating and diversion of funds involving homebuyers

title: Left Front to hold protest rally in Kolkata over 'attacks' on working people after poll results
content: "The LF constituents will hold the protest in the Esplanade area of the City's central district," a Front functionary said

title: Chandrababu Naidu directs officials to clear e-files within 24 hours, push digital governance
content: The Chief Minister says the speed of governance depends on the speed of file disposal, and asks departments to move almost entirely to e-office systems

title: Uttar Pradesh government sacks five doctors; orders action against 16 health officials for negligence
content: Five medical officers were dismissed for remaining absent from duty for a prolonged period without information and for staying away from medical work

title: Pedestrian killed in early-morning hit-and-run on Bengaluru’s MG Road
content: A preliminary police probe suggested that the deceased had used the washroom near the MG Road boulevard walkway and was crossing to the other side of the road when a reportedly speeding car struck him.

title: Excise policy: Delhi High Court to hear CBI plea against discharge of Kejriwal, Sisodia on May 11
content: Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma said she would pass the necessary order on the appointment on Monday (May 11) and begin hearing the matter on Tuesday (May 12).

title: Elderly woman killed in staged road accident for robbery in Bengaluru; five arrested
content: In a “cinematic-style” plan, the accused intentionally rammed a motorcycle into the victim, and then admitted her to a hospital. Meanwhile, one of the accomplices stole her house keys in a an attempt to commit theft.

title: Former Kerala DGP P.J. Alexander passes away at 92
content: Known for his academic excellence alongside his policing career, the 1961-batch IPS officer earned a doctorate on the subject of law enforcement and public order

title: KGBV contract teachers allege forced transfers; Samagra Shiksha says move is per contract
content: The KGBVs Teachers’ Union says principals and contract teachers were asked informally to apply for transfers. The Samagra Shiksha State Project Director says transfers are mandatory under the contract terms

title: Kozhikode to host ZSI hackathon on traditional biodiversity knowledge
content: The initiative seeks to bridge the gap between indigenous knowledge systems and modern technology to address critical challenges in global biodiversity conservation

title: BJP calls Sanjay Raut 'joker of Indian politics' over Donald Trump’s post on West Bengal polls
content: BJP spokesperson Shehzad Poonawalla alleged that SS-UBT MP Sanjay seems to “besmirch India's image for domestic political petty battles” and calls him the “joker of Indian politics"

title: Tamil Nadu government formation: Lone TVK cadre stages protest outside Lok Bhavan
content: Police identified the man as Suresh, an assistant of film director S.A. Chandrasekar, father of Mr. Vijay.

title: Writer T. Padmanabhan backs K.C. Venugopal for Kerala Chief Minister’s post
content: Padmanabhan says he has “close relations with many Congress leaders, but is personally closest” to Venugopal

title: NCC cadets test shooting skills at Combined Annual Training Camp in Tirupati
content: The 10-day camp at Alipiri, which began on May 5 and ends on May 14, includes drill, firing, obstacle course, debate, sports and cultural programmes

title: Akhilesh reaffirms support to TMC, DMK; takes dig at Congress
content: Akhilesh Yadav alleges that the Bengal polls witnessed "multi-layered mafia tactics" involving the BJP, the Election Commission and "underground elements.

title: India at Venice Biennale 2026 curtain raiser: Many voices, one resonance
content: From soil and thread to immersive myth and monumental sculpture, India’s Venice Biennale 2026 presence expands beyond the pavilion into a layered meditation on home and identity

title: TVK chief Vijay should be given opportunity to prove majority in Tamil Nadu Assembly: Jammu & Kashmir CM Omar Abdullah
content: “So, if we take the Supreme Court judgment, then the Tamil Nadu Lok Bhavan has no justification to stop this process” Jammu & Kashmir CM Omar Abdullah said

title: ECI publishes Assembly poll index cards, statistical reports in record 72 hrs
content: The data release covers all 830 Assembly constituencies where the election process concluded on May 6

title: Maharashtra Class 10 results out; over 92% students pass, girls outperform boys again
content: Girls secured an overall pass percentage of 94.96 as against boys' 89.56%, board chairman Trigun Kulkarni said

title: Smacks of low political opportunism: Mani Shankar Aiyar slams Congress’ TVK tie-up decision
content: Mani Shankar Aiyar said he cannot imagine the fathers of the Indian National Congress blessing such "politics of expediency"

title: GBA elections in Bengaluru to be held between June 14 and 24: State Election Commissioner
content: Inputs were also taken from the commissioners of the five corporations, who serve as ex-officio members, before moving ahead with the scheduling exercise.

title: Kerala polls: LDF will overcome setback and make strong comeback, says CPI(M) leader E.P. Jayarajan
content: In a Facebook post, Jayarajan says the scale of the defeat suffered by Left Democratic Front in Assembly election was “completely unexpected”

title: Can't be called backstabbing, what's the issue if like-minded parties forming govt in Tamil Nadu: Congress
content: The Congress, a long-time ally of the DMK, announced its support to actor-politician Vijay's TVK to form the government in Tamil Nadu and severed ties with the Dravidian major

title: BJP won’t support any coalition formation in Tamil Nadu: Nainar Nagenthran
content: The BJP, as part of the AIADMK-led National Democratic Alliance, contested 27 seats. The party’s nominee M. Bhojarajan won by a slim margin of 976 votes

title: CUET-UG advisory on dress code, permissible items released
content: The advisory further stated that white light clothing is preferred and candidates may wear woollens if required, subject to reporting early for frisking

title: India faces no immediate public health threat from hantavirus: NIV chief
content: Hantaviruses are mainly transmitted to humans through contact with infected rodents or their excreta such as saliva, urine and faeces, he told PTI

title: Left’s declining electoral strength a major concern, hopeful of revival, says D. Raja
content: The CPI general secretary said his party would soon decide its stand on whom to support in Tamil Nadu, and warned that the BJP forming a government in West Bengal posed challenges not just for the state but for the entire country.

title: Former IPS officer’s wife found dead at Jubilee Hills residence in Hyderabad, Nepal gang role suspected
content: Preliminary findings suggest that the gang gained entry into the house with the alleged assistance of a woman domestic help

title: Delhi tops crimes against foreigners, among Union Territories in 2024, confirms National Crime Records Bureau
content: Together, the Union Territories registered 67 such cases in the year, with Delhi alone contributing 64 cases, Jammu and Kashmir reported two cases, while the Andaman and Nicobar Islands recorded one case

title: Watch: Vijay won, but why can’t he become Chief Minister yet?
content: For the first time since 1952, Tamil Nadu has delivered a fractured mandate in an Assembly election. The man behind this turn of events is cinema superstar Vijay, who not only entered politics but also stunned the two Dravidian rivals, the DMK and AIADMK. But Vijay's TVK managed only 108 seats on its own, short of the majority mark of 118. Vijay will need the support of smaller parties to form the government and with the Congress in the mix, it poses a very interesting situation. So, what are the scenarios? What role does the Governor play?

title: West Bengal tops States in crimes by foreigners; Bangladeshis top accused: NCRB
content: The National Crime Records Bureau data showed that crimes against foreigners rose from 238 cases in 2023 to 257 in 2024

title: 'Bar has much to offer': CJI Kant shares anecdote as he dismisses lawyer's plea for revaluation
content: The CJI recounted his time as a final-year law student when he, too, wanted to become a judicial officer, had cleared the written test, and was to appear for the interview

title: In just four months of 2026, Tamil Nadu records 2.63 lakh dog bites, 17 deaths
content: A total of 62,000 dog bite cases were recorded each in January and February, and it rose to 71,000 in March and dipping slightly to 68,000 in April; half of last year’s deaths has been recorded in just four months this year

title: Srikakulam pushes Badi Pilustondi drive to lift admissions in government schools
content: The district administration is worried about a possible drop in admissions in 2026-27 as corporate schools expand into semi-urban areas with bus facilities

title: Telangana’s 2BHK dreams caught between delays, distance and vacancies
content: Several beneficiaries of Telangana’s double-bedroom housing scheme continue to wait for their allotted homes as construction remains incomplete. For several low-income families, rising rents and cost of living have deepened financial stress, turning the dream of a permanent house into an urgent necessity. But in a contrasting trend, several allotted flats in Hyderabad remain unoccupied. What steps is the government taking to complete pending works and improve occupancy in the housing colonies? K. Shiva Shanker, P. Laxma Reddy and P. Sridhar uncover the ground realities

title: CPI, CPI(M) meetings begin to decide on extending support for government formation in Tamil Nadu
content: The Viduthalai Chiruthaigal Katchi’s decision on the issue would depend on the outcome of the meetings of the Left parties

title: Tamil Nadu government formation: After Semmalai, AIADMK MP Thambi Durai too says Palaniswami will come back to power
content: The AIADMK veteran said during the election tour of the general secretary, the public sentiment was evident in favour of his party’s return to power.

title: Dead foetus kept in womb ‘for 22 hours’ in Maharashtra’s Beed; health dept. forms committee to probe
content: Family of the woman has alleged that the doctor attempted to hide the negligence and gain financial benefits

title: Amit Shah arrives in Kolkata to attend BJP's legislature party meeting, swearing in of new govt
content: Mr. Shah was received at the Netaji Subhash Chandra Bose International Airport by BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari

title: India pushes for inclusive migration, stronger anti-trafficking laws at United Nations General Assembly
content: Kirti Singh highlighted the various extensive initiatives undertaken by India, including the use of digital technologies, to support its nationals abroad

title: Sooryavanshi compared with Bethell; Ex-RCB star says these two will be 'unstoppable'
content: Cricket legend AB de Villiers has drawn a striking parallel between England's Jacob Bethell and India's 15-year-old sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, predicting both left-handers will become future superstars. While Sooryavanshi is dazzling in IPL 2026 with Rajasthan Royals, Bethell, now with RCB, is showing immense potential. De Villiers believes their natural talent, coupled with experience, will make them unstoppable.

title: The Vijay question: Should governor ascertain majority before inviting govt formation?
content: Actor-politician Vijay's TVK party emerged as the single-largest force in Tamil Nadu, winning 108 seats. Despite this, Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar has paused an invitation to form the government, citing an "unestablished majority." This has triggered a constitutional showdown, with legal experts debating the governor's discretionary powers and the necessity of a floor test before an invitation.

title: Cloudflare, whose CEO personally sends offer letter, is cutting 1,100 roles
content: Cybersecurity giant Cloudflare is laying off over 1,100 employees globally, citing a major reorganization for the 'agentic AI era'. The company's internal AI usage has surged dramatically, prompting a rethink of its structure to enhance customer value and speed. Departing staff receive industry-leading severance packages, including pay through 2026 and extended healthcare.

title: Stock market: Which are top 10 gainers and losers on NSE & BSE today? Check list
content: Indian equity markets experienced a downturn Friday, with both Sensex and Nifty closing lower. Renewed military tensions near the Strait of Hormuz dampened investor sentiment, leading to profit-booking across various sectors. While IT stocks showed resilience, PSU banks were significantly impacted. Global markets also reflected cautious sentiment.

title: Suvendu Adhikari PA murder case: No trace of gunmen, police draw blank from seized bike
content: Police in West Bengal's border districts are intensifying searches, suspecting professional shooters from neighboring states in the assassination of BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari's aide, Chandranath Rath. Investigators are also exploring the involvement of local criminals who may have provided logistical support.

title: 'You have 10 working days to voluntarily recognise unions': Google UK staff tells company
content: Google's DeepMind employees are demanding union recognition amid concerns over their AI technology's use by the US and Israeli militaries. Workers seek a commitment against developing AI weapons and surveillance tools, urging management to voluntarily recognize their unions or face legal action. The move highlights growing anxieties about AI's ethical implications and its potential for harm.

title: 'Premature and misplaced’: Sanjay Raut writes to Trump over Bengal win message to PM Modi
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) leader Sanjay Raut criticized Donald Trump's congratulations to PM Modi on the West Bengal election results, calling them premature and misplaced. Raut asserted that state elections are an internal matter and alleged the polls were conducted amid fear and systemic pressure, questioning the Election Commission's impartiality.

title: Kerala CM face: BJP drops leaked Congress list with 'KC' markings
content: With the LDF's victory in Kerala, the race for Chief Minister heats up. A leaked list, shared by the BJP, suggests K C Venugopal might be favored by a majority of Congress MLAs. However, one MLA claims his preference was omitted, raising questions about the consultation process. V D Satheesan and Ramesh Chennithala are also contenders.

title: Coinbase reports service issues, points finger at AWS cloud outage
content: A cooling issue at an Amazon Web Services data center in Northern Virginia caused widespread service disruptions. Cryptocurrency exchange Coinbase and derivatives giant CME Group were among the affected. AWS confirmed increased temperatures in a single data center impacted services. Efforts to restore cooling capacity led to extended outages for some customers. The primary issue is now resolved.

title: 'Changed political circumstances’: DMK seeks separate seating in LS after break-up with Cong
content: DMK MP Kanimozhi has requested Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla to change parliamentary seating arrangements, moving DMK MPs away from Congress. This follows the end of their two-decade alliance in Tamil Nadu after the recent poll results. The DMK accused Congress of betrayal for supporting another party to form the government, warning of widespread electoral consequences.

title: SBI Q4 results: Largest lender records 6% rise in profit; share price tumbles 7%
content: State Bank of India reported a 6% rise in Q4 FY26 net profit to Rs 19,684 crore, driven by improved asset quality and lower bad loans. Despite this, total income saw a slight decline, and net interest margins compressed, leading to a drop in operating profit and a subsequent fall in SBI's stock price.

title: 'Organisational arrogance, grassroots disconnect': Rumblings of revolt in TMC after poll debacle
content: Trinamool Congress faces internal turmoil as former MLA Raj Chakraborty quits politics, citing the end of his tenure. This follows public criticism from ex-ministers and functionaries blaming leadership arrogance and factionalism for the poll defeat. Several leaders have voiced discontent over party decisions and alleged pressure from within.

title: Hantavirus scare on cruise ship: ICMR says no immediate threat to India
content: Two Indian nationals aboard a cruise ship have tested positive for hantavirus, but health officials assure there's no immediate threat to India.  The virus, primarily spread by rodents, does not transmit easily between humans. Global health authorities are monitoring the situation, with the public risk deemed very low.

title: 'Let us know': VCK attacks Vijay's 'arrogance' over WhatsApp support messages
content: Viduthalai Chiruthaigal Katchi (VCK) criticizes actor-politician Vijay's TVK for seeking government support via WhatsApp messages instead of direct consultations. VCK questions Vijay's political handling and accuses his party of misguiding him and exploiting his popularity while also criticizing TVK's stance on dynastic politics and resort politics.

title: Dig at Cong? Akhilesh shares Mamata, Stalin photos with 'not ones who abandon' post
content: Akhilesh's remark was in the backdrop of the Congress snapping ties with ally DMK in Tamil Nadu and extending support to actor Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), which has added to rancour in the INDIA bloc. The 2026 assembly elections in Tamil Nadu and West Bengal have cast a foreboding shadow on the NDA's rival bloc as  Mamata and Stalin were two powerful regional leaders in the anti-BJP camp.

title: Sairaj Bahutule to take charge as India’s spin bowling coach after IPL 2026 stint with PBKS
content: Former India spinner Sairaj Bahutule is poised to become the Indian men's team's spin-bowling coach for the next two years.  With extensive experience, including with the Punjab Kings and at the NCA, Bahutule's appointment aims to bolster India's spin attack. His tenure is expected to commence with the Afghanistan series in June. The Team India coaching setup currently includes batting coach Sitanshu Kotak, fast-bowling coach Morne Morkel, fielding coach T Dilip and assistant coach Ryan ten Doeschate, with Bahutule now set to fill the spin-bowling coach’s role.

title: Kohli’s IPL ducks tale: Why multiple dismissals for nought have often hurt his season
content: Virat Kohli's IPL 2026 campaign hit a rare snag as Lucknow Super Giants pacer Prince Yadav dismissed him for a two-ball duck. This marked Kohli's first IPL duck in 1110 days, a stunning event given his legendary status. The delivery itself was hailed as 'ball of the tournament', leaving fans and experts in awe of the rare dismissal.

title: Crude timing: $7 billion 'unusual' oil bets placed ahead of Iran war announcements
content: Massive oil market bets, totaling up to $7 billion, were placed just before significant US President Trump announcements concerning Iran.  Traders executed large sell orders minutes before key political developments, leading to substantial price drops.  This unusual timing and scale have triggered regulatory scrutiny, with investigators examining potential insider trading concerns.

title: Dhoni tops taxpayer list as Jharkhand-Bihar see Rs 20,000 crore tax collection
content: Former India captain MS Dhoni has been revealed as the highest individual taxpayer from Jharkhand and Bihar combined for the 2025-26 financial year. The Principal Chief Commissioner of Income Tax confirmed Dhoni's top position, though the exact tax amount remains undisclosed. This achievement highlights Dhoni's significant financial contribution to the region.

title: Explained: Why Bombay HC upheld 2018 acquittal in 2005 fake encounter case of Sohrabuddin Shaikh
content: The Bombay High Court upheld a trial court's acquittal of 21 police officers and others in the Sohrabuddin Shaikh, Kausar Bi, and Tulsiram Prajapati alleged fake encounter case. The court found the prosecution failed to establish abduction, conspiracy, or murder, deeming the evidence insufficient to overturn the initial verdict. Appeals by Shaikh's brothers were dismissed.

title: BCCI warns IPL teams over honey-trap risks, tightens hotel room access rules
content: The BCCI has issued a stern warning to IPL franchises regarding 'targeted compromise and honey-trapping,' enhancing security and access protocols. Stricter rules now govern hotel room access, unauthorised departures, and accreditation display. Franchise owners face new restrictions on interacting with players during matches, and vaping is strictly prohibited, with severe penalties for violations.

title: Pakistan takes a risky gamble, skips purchasing spot LNG, bets Hormuz tensions will ease
content: Pakistan is foregoing urgent LNG purchases, betting on a swift de-escalation of Middle East tensions and the timely arrival of cheaper Qatari shipments. This risky strategy comes as the country faces a significant gas deficit and widespread electricity outages, exacerbated by disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Explainer: Can hantavirus outbreak become Covid 2.0?
content: Health authorities are urgently tracing passengers from the MV Hondius cruise ship following a hantavirus outbreak that has claimed three lives. The Andes strain, capable of human-to-human transmission, was identified, prompting isolation and contact tracing. While not comparable to COVID-19, the virus requires close contact to spread.

title: 'Raja Shivaji' BO collection day 8: Riteish Deshmukh film sets sight on Rs 75 crore on second weekend
content: 'Raja Shivaji', directed by and starring Riteish Deshmukh as Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, has delivered the greatest opening week in Marathi cinema history with Rs. 52.65 crore India net in Week 1 and is chasing the Rs. 75 crore India net target as its second weekend begins. Released on Maharashtra Day (May 1, 2026), the Rs. 100 crore historical epic — the most expensive Marathi film ever made features Sanjay Dutt, Abhishek Bachchan, Vidya Balan, Genelia Deshmukh and the debut of Rahyl Deshmukh, with Salman Khan's still-viral surprise cameo as Jiva Mahala continuing to drive footfalls.

title: Retired IPS officer’s wife found murdered inside Jubilee Hills home, domestic help under lens
content: Tragedy struck in Hyderabad's Jubilee Hills as the wife of former IPS officer Vinay Ranjan Ray was found murdered. Authorities suspect a recently hired domestic worker from Nepal and her accomplices, who allegedly stole valuables and fled. The victim was found on the first floor of the family's residence. Police are actively pursuing the absconding suspects.

title: Delhi Capitals still haunted by 'mental scar' of GT loss: Ex-India cricketer
content: Former India spinner Murali Kartik feels Delhi Capitals' IPL campaign was derailed by a one-run loss to Gujarat Titans, leaving them mentally scarred. Despite a decent squad, DC has struggled since that defeat, experiencing extreme results. Now seventh, they face a resurgent Kolkata Knight Riders, who are in winning form.

title: India is making its 'dumb bombs' smart: What DRDO’s TARA glide kit means for the IAF
content: India's indigenous glide weapon system, TARA, achieved a successful maiden flight, transforming unguided bombs into precision-guided munitions. Developed by DRDO and tested from an IAF Jaguar, TARA offers enhanced accuracy and stand-off capability at low cost. This development aligns with global trends, boosting India's affordable precision strike power and airpower.

title: US strikes down Trump's 10% tariffs — How should India proceed now?
content: US courts have again blocked Trump's 10% global tariffs, deeming them unauthorized. This ruling, following earlier tariff invalidations, signals a return to WTO's MFN rates. For India, this policy instability raises concerns, prompting advice to pause Free Trade Agreement talks until US trade systems stabilize, preventing one-sided deals.

title: BTech student chased, stabbed multiple times over relationship in Hyderabad, dies
content: A 23-year-old BTech student was brutally stabbed to death in Secunderabad's Chilakalguda, allegedly due to a long-standing love affair. The victim was attacked by six unidentified men on motorcycles. Protests erupted as the victim's family accused the woman's family of orchestrating the murder.

title: Rs 27 crore, one big question - What has happened to Rishabh Pant in T20s?
content: Rishabh Pant’s unbeaten 32 helped Lucknow Super Giants beat Royal Challengers Bengaluru by nine runs in Lucknow, but questions over his T20 form continue to grow. Once seen as one of India’s brightest white-ball stars, Pant has struggled for consistency in recent IPL seasons despite becoming the league’s most expensive player at Rs 27 crore. His falling returns in T20 cricket, changing role at LSG and growing competition for India spots have added to the pressure, with Irfan Pathan also admitting the wicketkeeper-batter is under scrutiny.

title: Virat shares heartwarming moment with Prince after pacer removes him for duck
content: Virat Kohli shared a warm moment with young LSG pacer Prince Yadav after being dismissed by a brilliant nip-backer. Yadav revealed Kohli himself had advised him to stick to that length after a previous match. Despite the dismissal, Kohli appeared relaxed and appreciative of the youngster's performance.

title: Horrific murder on CCTV: Woman chased, stabbed to death by husband in Telangana
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Suryapet district as a 24-year-old housewife was brutally stabbed to death by her estranged husband at a tea stall. The victim, Sirisha, had left her abusive husband, Manideep, over marital disputes. Despite police intervention for counselling, Manideep ambushed and fatally attacked her in public, leading to her death.
